title: Selenius VII

{{Quote|Seems that he marched in here last year and announced to all and sundry that ''he'' was going to be the one to capture the infamous Han Solo.|A bounty hunter in "Arc of Fire" Bar & Grill, on Renlo|<ref name="GG10" />}}
'''Selenius VII''', also referred to as '''Selenius''', was an astronomical object that lay in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]], along the [[D'Aelgoth Trade Route]]. A busy [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], it was the location of the establishment known as [["Arc of Fire" Bar & Grill]].

Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Renlo]] announced at "Arc of Fire" his intention to capture the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. [[Standard month/Legends|Months]] later, Renlo returned to the Selenius establishment defeated and proceeded to spend his [[Standard day/Legends|days]] there lamenting his failure.

==Description==
Selenius VII,<ref name="GG10" /> also known simply as Selenius, was an astronomical object located in the [[Selenius system/Legends|Selenius system]] of the [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]], a part of the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The [[D'Aelgoth Trade Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked it to the [[Ogem system/Legends|Ogem system]] as well as the [[Kassido system/Legends|Kassido system]] of the neighboring [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Fifteen months later, he's back with 'nay but the torn and bloodied tunic on his back. ''[…]'' That's him over in the corner, sweeping up glass and mutterin' to himself, 'Almost had 'im,' over and over again.|A bounty hunter, on Renlo|<ref name="GG10" />}}
At some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' is set before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Since "[[Almost Had 'Im]]" establishes that [[Renlo]] first entered [["Arc of Fire" Bar & Grill]] at least fifteen [[Standard month/Legends|months]] prior to the conversation taking place in the narrative, the former event must have happened at some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Renlo]] entered [["Arc of Fire" Bar & Grill]] at Selenius VII and announced to everyone present his intent to capture [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]],<ref name="GG10" /> a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] with a significant [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Following the announcement, Renlo bought beverages for all the patrons of the establishment and then left to pursue the smuggler.<ref name="GG10" />

Fifteen [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, the bounty [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] returned to "Arc of Fire," having been defeated by his quarry and having lost both his equipment and his [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Renlo spent the following [[Standard day/Legends|days]] at "Arc of Fire" lamenting his near-capture of the smuggler. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], [[Anonymous hunter|another bounty hunter]] mentioned Renlo's experiences in a conversation held at the Selenius VII establishment.<ref name="GG10" />

==Inhabitants==
Selenius VII was a busy [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. The [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[Ancient Houses]] of the Juvex [[Sector/Legends|sector]] maintained numerous [[Spy/Legends|spies]] at the celestial body.<ref name="TEA" />

==Locations==
The establishment known as "Arc of Fire" Bar & Grill was located at Selenius VII.<ref name="GG10" />

==Behind the scenes==
Selenius VII was introduced in "[[Almost Had 'Im]]," a short companion narrative included in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''. The book was authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG10" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' provided a shortened name for the celestial body and placed it in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
